entries.summary: Phase 7 Documentation Verification complete. 3 tasks: README/ARCHITECTURE (7 findings), CONFIGURATION/USAGE (13 findings), REMEDIATION_REPORT (3 findings). Total 23 findings. CONFIGURATION.md is the most stale doc with 8 contradicted claims including settings path, backend priority, defaults.

metadata.plan_id: Comprehensive Codebase QA Review — AI-Hardened Edition

lessons_learned: CONFIGURATION.md has the most documentation drift of any file — 8 contradicted claims in a single document. Default values, paths, and env vars are all stale.
Phantom env vars (RAG_TOP_P, RAG_DO_SAMPLE) documented but never read by code are a common documentation smell — they create false confidence in configuration coverage.
The /ask/stream endpoint documented in ARCHITECTURE.md but non-existent in code is a significant fabrication — users following the architecture doc would implement a feature that doesn't exist.
REMEDIATION_REPORT.md is well-written but has stealth changes — Fix D2 was described as 'incremental' but the limitation section reveals it's still O(N) per call.
Documentation verification should run early in future audits — many doc findings could have informed source code audit priorities.
